rationale: Both correctly diagnose the root cause (base tokenizer missing extended ratio tokens, config forcing two-stage topology) and cite the same fix/workaround verified by FayeSpica, matching the ground truth. X is more precisely grounded (specific line ranges, quotes yaml headers, notes the downstream _all_ratio_ids builder handles an empty slice cleanly) and explicitly honors Gaohan123's actual closing request to track the hardening/docs work in a separate follow-up issue rather than in this one. Y's response is marred by a raw, duplicated 'draft_answer' dump (the same answer pasted twice) and the final answer is truncated mid-sentence ('Document the Base-v'), and it never acknowledges the maintainer's explicit ask to defer follow-up work to a new issue, making it less complete and less polished as a maintainer-style reply.
